title: Canvas & viewport: Metal-backed view, zoom/pan, selection
goal: A canvas that feels like Preview/Pixelmator: instant zoom/pan, crisp at any scale, marquee + layer selection.

tasks.title: Replace ScrollView+Image with NSViewRepresentable Metal/CALayer canvas
tasks.notes: Viewport (PhotonzCore, 10 tests) owns all camera math: fit-never-upscales, zoom-to-anchor, per-axis clamp (center when smaller, edge-clamp when larger), center-preserving resize. CanvasNSView (app) is a flipped layer-backed NSView mirroring Viewport into a CALayer; nearest-neighbor magnification at zoom >= 2. Verified headlessly via CALayer.render(in:) pixel harness (no Screen Recording permission on this machine — screencapture fails; use the layer-render approach for visual checks). Gotcha: compound integer-literal exprs inside #expect infer as Int and never equal CGFloat — pre-type expected values.

tasks.title: Pinch zoom, scroll pan, ⌘0 fit / ⌘1 100%, zoom-to-cursor
tasks.notes: CanvasNSView overrides scrollWheel (pan, 10x multiplier for non-precise wheels), magnify (zoom anchored at cursor), smartMagnify (fit <-> 100%/200% toggle). Gestures apply locally first (zero-latency echo) then notify AppState via onViewportChange. View menu: ⌘= / ⌘- / ⌘0 fit / ⌘1 actual size (PhotonzApp commands). Scroll-pan verified headlessly with a synthesized CGEvent scroll NSEvent; magnify events can't be constructed headlessly — math is Viewport-tested.

tasks.title: Marquee selection with marching-ants overlay (canvas coords in core, tested)
tasks.notes: MarqueeDrag (PhotonzCore/Marquee.swift, 13 tests): doc-coord drag → standardized/⇧-square/canvas-clamped rect; isClick(atZoom:) keeps the 4pt click tolerance in view points; Geometry.pixelAligned snaps committed selections to the pixel grid. CanvasNSView owns the live drag (zero-latency, same pattern as pan/zoom) and two CAShapeLayers for ants (solid white under animated black dashes, lineDashPhase 0→8 @0.4s). Click or Esc deselects; selection lives in AppState (doc coords), survives zoom/pan, cleared on canvas-size change. Verified headlessly: swiftc-compiled harness (CanvasView.swift + core sources + synthesized NSEvents into an offscreen NSWindow, CALayer.render(in:) → PNG — note render output is vertically flipped). Gotcha: NSViewRepresentable echo must not stomp the live drag — apply() ignores incoming selection while marquee != nil.

tasks.title: Layer hit-testing and click-to-select, drag-to-move with snapping
tasks.acceptance: Hit-test order is top-down, respects isLocked/isVisible; snap to canvas edges/centers at 8px screen tolerance.
tasks.notes: Core (11 tests): Layer.contains(canvasPoint:) inverts the render transform around the frame center; PhotonzDocument.hitTest is top-down and skips invisible/locked; Snapping.snapFrameOrigin attracts layer edges/centers to canvas edges/center with tolerance in SCREEN points (8/zoom doc points). DECISION: withBaseImage now locks the background (Photoshop convention) so clicks on it marquee instead of moving the base image. App: pointer-modal, no tool state — hit → select + move drag (accent outline, yellow snap guides spanning the doc); miss → marquee + deselect. Move preview renders via AppState.previewLayerMove (scheduler submit, no history); mouse-up commits ONE undo step; Esc mid-drag commits the start origin (History no-ops it) to reset the preview render. Click-without-move (<4 screen pts) selects without committing. Verified with the same headless NSEvent harness as 2.3.

tasks.title: Selection handles (resize from corners/edges, ⇧ preserves aspect)
Gesture preview pipeline: cheap transform preview during drag, real render on commit
